mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is in the lower tail of its historical distribution.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2018-12 indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 7.0% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% as of 2018-12 is above 7.0% threshold
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 1.0 million units annualized, jointly signaling weakening consumer demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as both demand proxies deteriorate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1000 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 914k units as of 2018-11 is below 1.0M threshold
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power (Rank IC) than either proxy alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months signals supply-side contraction and profit margin compression. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 as of 2018-12 shows recent weakness
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k per month while unemployment rate remains elevated above 7.0%, jointly indicating labor market stagnation. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as the market reprices growth and recession probability upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135.9M and UNRATE at 7.6% as of 2018-12
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (Rank IC improvement < 0.02) over single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate is above 2.0%, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns as the Fed is expected to pause or cut rates, supporting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.83 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2018-12
regime_hint: monetary policy restrictiveness relative to inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy (Sharpe ratio < 0.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 900k units, jointly signaling demand-side recession risk. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns as investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 900 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13725.7B and HOUST at 914k as of 2018-11
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either proxy alone (Rank IC differential < 0.01) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth remains below 1.0% year-over-year while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is below 2.0%, indicating weak demand-constrained supply with limited pricing power. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as the market interprets low inflation as dovish Fed policy support.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.67 and CPIAUCSL at 231.83 as of 2018-12
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in low-inflation, low-growth regimes do not exceed baseline returns by at least 25 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).
